title: Refractive index
short: How much a medium slows light, n = c/v — a dimensionless number ≥ 1.
full:
The refractive index n of a material is the ratio of the speed of light in vacuum to its speed in that material, n = c/v. It is always at least 1 (vacuum is exactly 1; air ≈ 1.0003; water ≈ 1.33; glass ≈ 1.5).

The higher the index, the slower light travels and the more it bends when crossing a boundary, as described by Snell's law, n₁·sin θ₁ = n₂·sin θ₂.

Being a ratio, the refractive index has no units.
